summary: DHH defends the Canadian trucker convoy's protest against vaccine mandates, arguing their peaceful demonstration is a legitimate exercise of democratic rights. He criticizes progressive commentators for condemning peaceful truckers while having cheered violent 2020 protests, and accuses Canadian politicians of refusing to engage with the substantive argument. He points to Denmark's approach of rejecting vaccine mandates as evidence the truckers' position is reasonable. He argues that efforts to cut off donations, seize fuel, and label protesters as extremists are designed to provoke escalation that would justify a crackdown.
key_points: Progressive commentators are hypocritical for condemning peaceful trucker protests while having supported violent 2020 protests
Canadian politicians refuse to engage with the merits of the anti-mandate argument, instead resorting to labels like white supremacist and insurrectionist
Denmark's health authority rejected vaccine mandates, showing the truckers' position aligns with mainstream international policy
Efforts to cut off donations and fuel appear designed to provoke desperation and escalation to justify a crackdown
Over 90% of Canadian truckers are already vaccinated, making the mandate both punitive and ineffective given omicron's spread patterns
money_quotes: How dare these truckers continue to exercise their basic political rights to freedom of assembly, association, and speech?!
How does one square the condemnation of peaceful political protests on the one hand with cheering on violent political protests on the other? By any means necessary, of course!
What's fascinating about the Canadian trucker protest is the unwillingness of politicians to engage with the merits of the argument.
Forcing vaccines upon truckers needing to cross the border for work is a gross overreach of state power, and utterly unwarranted by the state of the pandemic.
Without support to sustain the peaceful protests, maybe the truckers will turn desperate, and we can have the escalation needed to justify the hammer?
The right to peaceful protest does not have an expiration date.
A healthy democracy will engage with protesters to hear their claims and consider their voice within the guardrails of peaceful expression.
key_insight: Democratic governments that refuse to engage with the substance of peaceful protests and instead try to delegitimize protesters through labels and resource deprivation are undermining the very democratic norms they claim to defend.
